CompScience VMS Device Addendum

Effective upon acceptance

Kinetic Incorporated d/b/a CompScience · 2021 Fillmore Street #195, San Francisco, CA 94115

This VMS Device Addendum (“Addendum”) governs your receipt and use of the CompScience Video Management System device (“Device”) and supplements the CompScience Terms of Service and Privacy Policy, which are incorporated herein by reference. In the event of a conflict between this Addendum and those policies, this Addendum controls with respect to the Device. Capitalized terms not defined here have the meanings given in the Terms of Service. Please review those policies before accepting.

1. Device Ownership & Return

The Device remains the sole property of CompScience at all times. You acquire no ownership interest. Upon expiration or termination of your service agreement for any reason, you must return the Device in good working condition, ordinary wear and tear excepted, within 30 days. Failure to return the Device within that period will result in a replacement charge equal to the then-current list price of the Device.

2. Device Damage or Loss

CompScience will replace one Device per contract year at no charge in the event of damage or loss occurring while the Device is in your custody, provided you notify CompScience promptly and cooperate in good faith with any reasonable inquiry. If a second or subsequent replacement is required within the same contract year, CompScience will invoice you the then-current cost of the replacement Device. Ordinary wear and tear is excluded from this provision.

3. Purpose of Video Access

By accepting this Addendum, you authorize CompScience to access and retrieve video from your facility solely to deliver the workplace safety analysis services described in your service agreement. No other use of your video is permitted without your separate written consent.

CompScience will not use your video footage to train AI or machine learning models, resell it to third parties, or transfer it outside the CompScience environment except as required by applicable law or as expressly set forth in the Privacy Policy.

4. Camera Exclusions

You may designate specific cameras as excluded at any time by providing written notice to your CompScience account representative. CompScience will not configure, access, or retrieve footage from excluded cameras. Exclusions take effect within two (2) business days of written notice and remain in force for the duration of this Addendum unless modified by mutual written agreement.

5. Access Timing

CompScience will only access or record video during time windows mutually agreed upon in your service configuration. Any change to agreed access windows requires written consent from both parties.

6. Facial Blurring

CompScience’s platform does not perform facial recognition or collect biometric identifiers. If required by applicable law or your internal policy, CompScience can enable upload-pipeline facial blurring so that video is processed to obscure facial features before storage on CompScience servers. Contact your account representative to enable this feature.

7. Network Connection & Client Control

You are requested to maintain the Device’s network connection to enable security patches and software updates. You may disconnect the Device or request that CompScience disable video capture at any time, without providing a reason. You acknowledge that doing so will pause delivery of Services and may interrupt security and software updates while disconnected.

You agree to cooperate in good faith with CompScience during initial installation and configuration of the Device.

8. Security

CompScience maintains SOC 2–compliant security controls with respect to video data it accesses. CompScience is not responsible for security failures originating in your network, internal systems, or third-party infrastructure, including your existing camera hardware or network access controls. You remain solely responsible for the physical security of the Device while in your custody.

9. Workplace Surveillance Compliance

You represent and warrant that prior to activating the Device you have complied with all applicable federal, state, and local laws governing workplace video surveillance, including any obligations to post notices or inform employees that video recording is in use. CompScience’s platform does not identify individuals and does not collect facial recognition or biometric data; however, notification requirements under surveillance laws are independent of whether identification occurs and remain your sole responsibility. CompScience assumes no liability for your compliance with such obligations.

10. Data Retention

CompScience retains video footage indefinitely by default as part of the Services. If you wish to request deletion of specific footage or establish a defined retention window, you may do so by submitting a written request to your account representative. CompScience will honor reasonable retention and deletion requests consistent with applicable law and as further described in the Privacy Policy.

11. Liability

The liability limitations and indemnification obligations set forth in Section 7 of the Terms of Service apply to this Addendum. CompScience is not liable for (a) security failures originating in your network, camera hardware, or internal systems; (b) misuse or unauthorized use of the Device by your personnel or third parties; or (c) your failure to comply with applicable workplace surveillance notice or consent requirements.

12. Governing Terms

Except as modified herein, all terms of the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy remain in full force and effect.

Acceptance

By clicking “I Accept” or otherwise indicating acceptance (electronically or in writing), you confirm that: (i) you have read and understand this Addendum and the incorporated Terms of Service and Privacy Policy; (ii) you have authority to bind the organization on whose behalf you are acting; and (iii) you agree to be bound by all terms herein.

Questions? Contact us at hello@compscience.com.
